behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is filter→ranker handoff: a high-score item can leak through if exclusion is not enforced after module interaction. In-process wiring of catalog filter + ranker catches this. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI model-output dependence or multi-agent sequence in the behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real filtering and ranking modules with an in-memory catalog containing high-relevance out-of-stock items; assert final ranked output contains none of those IDs.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: This is direct filter logic on inputs (blocked categories + products) and is reproducible inside one function/object boundary. Rejected Integration: no behavior text requiring cross-module state handoff. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: no real infra or AI dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the category-filter function with products spanning blocked/allowed categories and explicit user settings; assert blocked categories are removed.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: This is scoring/ranking algorithm behavior with fixed feature inputs; one local ranking function can expose weighting mistakes. Rejected Integration: behavior text targets ranking logic itself, not module handoff. Rejected Agentic: no real LLM/ML API call implied.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the ranking function a controlled candidate set where only some items have recent-browsing features; assert those items score/rank above zero-signal items.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: Stability depends on ordering + pagination modules interacting over same snapshot; bugs often appear at the boundary (e.g., non-stable sort before slicing). Rejected Unit: single-function test may miss cross-module ordering/slicing interaction. Rejected System: no required real OS/network/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Run ranking + sorting + paginator together against fixed in-memory data; request page 2 twice with unchanged snapshot and assert identical ordered IDs both times.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: This is an algorithmic constraint that can be validated in one ranking/diversification function with fixed inputs. Rejected Integration: behavior text does not require external module/state boundaries. Rejected Agentic: serendipity rule is deterministic business logic here, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke diversification/ranking logic with user-history categories and candidate pool; assert final top-N includes at least one item from an unseen category.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: Cold-start behavior usually depends on detector + fallback popularity source + rank assembly. Real failure is in module orchestration (empty-history handoff to fallback). Rejected Unit: single-function test can miss missing fallback wiring. Rejected System/Agentic: no required real OS/network or model-output quality in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Use real recommendation service wiring with a user profile having no history and an in-memory popularity provider; assert non-empty output and that ranking follows popularity inputs.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: This checks event capture + metadata enrichment + persistence interaction. In-process DB/repository is enough to catch mapping bugs. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network/subprocess/OS primitives. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate recommendation display and click events through the tracking service into a local test store; assert stored records contain exact product ID, displayed position, and generated timestamp per click.

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: This is a performance SLO tied to real runtime/CPU/memory behavior; in-process mocks can hide latency hot spots and are not trustworthy for the 200ms boundary. Rejected Integration per tie-breaker because concrete failure (algorithm passes with fake/stubbed data access but exceeds 200ms under real process conditions) is only caught at higher level. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no LLM step.
behaviors.test_description: Run the recommendation endpoint/service in a real process with a 100k-product dataset and production-like indexing/config; measure end-to-end latency across repeated calls and assert p95 (or required metric) is <200ms.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: Requires purchase-event update and subsequent ranking request to share state correctly; failure appears at state handoff boundary. Rejected Unit: isolated scorer test misses event ingestion/storage propagation failures. Rejected System: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute recommendation generation, record a purchase event for one recommended item, then request recommendations again through wired modules and assert that item’s rank is lowered.

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Determinism depends on full pipeline consistency (feature extraction, ordering, pagination/randomness controls), not just one function. Rejected Unit: single-function checks can miss non-deterministic behavior introduced by module composition. Rejected Agentic: behavior text describes deterministic recommender output, not LLM decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the end-to-end recommendation service twice for identical persisted user/catalog state with no writes between calls; assert identical ranked product IDs and order.
